commit.message:
content: add Dream of the Red Chamber / Zhu Cizhao authorship theory to Ming dynasty section

Add substantial new subsection covering the 2023 claim by researcher
Cao Dongfang that Dream of the Red Chamber was authored by Zhu Cizhao,
fourth son of the Chongzhen Emperor, rather than the conventionally
attributed Cao Xueqin. Content includes:

- Overview of the Zhu Cizhao theory and its four pillars of evidence
  (Jiaxu manuscript, palace objects, Kangxi-era dating, coded names)
- Historical biography of Zhu Cizhao (1632-1708) and his fate after
  the fall of Ming, including the Zhu San Taizi affair
- Reference photos of the 2023 Shandong Zhonglin Museum letter
- Balanced editorial note on the contested nature of the claim and
  mainstream Redology's position
- Related content entry in dynastiesData.json for the Ming page

commit.message:
VoronoiResult infrastructure for game-phase eval (Iter 16)

Enrich VoronoiTerritory BFS to return VoronoiResult struct with food
ownership (MyFood/OppFood) and partition detection (IsPartitioned) at
zero extra cost (~1048ns/0 allocs). Eval unchanged — constant-weight
food signals and root-level partition short-circuit were tested across
6 configs (28-51% vs Iter 14) and found harmful or neutral. Key finding:
food value varies by game phase, so flat weights create noise. Infrastructure
is ready for game-phase-aware eval in Iter 17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update 'backed by' to 'powered by' in founder section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add RewardContainer system: floating container drops and shatters on dummy death

New ARewardContainer actor with StaticMesh + GeometryCollection swap.
ArenaManager binds RewardDummy death to container activation.
Flow: dummy dies → container GC spawns → falls under gravity → impact probe
detects ground → GC shatters → reward actor spawns at impact point.

commit.message:
feat: replace countdown timer with static 2026 convergence callout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(webhook): process rich first messages instead of forcing generic greeting

When the client's first message already contains a professional name + date
(e.g. "Tem horário com Lipe amanhã depois das 17?"), the AI would ignore
everything and just say "Como posso te ajudar?" due to the shouldGreet override.

- Added richFirstMessage detection: if TS layer extracted prof + date from
  the very first message, suppress shouldGreet so AI can actually answer
- Added time-preference pre-extraction: "depois das 17" → preferredTime:"17:00"
  so AI knows to filter/suggest slots from that time onward
- Added preferredTime to CONTEXTO ATUAL shown to AI when no specific time is set
- greetedAt is still set so the next turn won't trigger greeting again

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(vdbe,btree,core,mvcc,parser,pager): UPDATE FROM codegen, index seek fix, CAST/affinity corrections, and performance improvements

VDBE codegen (fsqlite-vdbe):
- Implement full UPDATE ... FROM codegen with nested-loop join strategy.
  Add codegen_update_from() generating OpenWrite for target, OpenRead for
  FROM source, dual Rewind/Next loops, WHERE filter, and delete+insert
  update pattern. Previously returned Unsupported error.
- Add SecondaryScan struct and secondary field to ScanCtx for multi-table
  column resolution in UPDATE ... FROM expressions. emit_expr now falls
  through to secondary table when column not found in primary.
- Add emit_column_from_cursor() helper for emitting Column/Rowid opcodes
  against a specific cursor.
- Fix If/IfNot opcodes to use integer truncation (to_integer) instead of
  float comparison, matching C SQLite's sqlite3VdbeIntValue() semantics
  where 0.5 is falsy.
- Fix sql_rem (modulo) to use f64 fmod semantics instead of integer
  truncation, matching C SQLite's OP_Remainder behavior.
- Switch HashMap/HashSet to hashbrown in engine.rs for performance.
- Update test from rejection assertion to positive nested-loop validation.

B-tree cursor (fsqlite-btree):
- Split index_seek into index_seek (for queries) and index_seek_for_insert
  (for mutations). The query path now handles right-edge leaf falloff by
  using advance_next to find the successor, while the insert path keeps
  the cursor anchored at the leaf for correct insertion context.
- Add proptest for index B-tree invariants with random insert/delete
  sequences (up to 2000 ops), verifying sorted order and element count.

Connection and core (fsqlite-core):
- Rewrite implicit_index_definitions_from_create_table_sql to parse AST
  ColumnConstraintKind directly instead of going through compat_persist
  string parsing. More robust for UNIQUE and PRIMARY KEY detection.
- Schema reload now returns an error instead of silently skipping when an
  index cannot be reconstructed, preventing silent data corruption.
- Fix type_to_affinity() priority order to match SQLite spec section 3.1:
  INT > TEXT/CHAR/CLOB > BLOB > REAL/FLOA/DOUB > NUMERIC (was previously
  checking REAL before TEXT).
- Rewrite apply_cast() to use SQLite affinity substring matching instead
  of exact type name matching. Add parse_integer_prefix and
  parse_float_prefix for SQLite-compatible prefix extraction from strings.
- Simplify GROUP_CONCAT and sqlite_value_to_text to use SqliteValue::to_text().
- Pass HashMap conversions via into_iter().collect() for autoincrement
  and column defaults (hashbrown compatibility).
- Add hashbrown workspace dependency.

MVCC write coordinator (fsqlite-mvcc):
- Simplify compat_commit_batch: eagerly reserve pages in the commit index
  during Phase 1 validation (under the write lock) instead of deferring
  to Phase 4. This eliminates intra-batch conflict tracking and the
  separate batch_page_owner HashMap, reducing code by ~70 lines.
- Remove now-unused validate_fcw_set, update_commit_index methods.
- Move allocate_commit_seq to a more logical position in the impl block.
- Drop the index write lock before Phase 3 I/O for better concurrency.

Parser (fsqlite-parser):
- Optimize lexer whitespace skipping: batch-scan whitespace length first,
  then advance_by() once instead of character-by-character advance().
- Add Scope::has_cte() for recursive CTE visibility checking through
  parent scope chain (previously only checked current scope).
- Fix ORDER BY alias resolution to use add_alias with pseudo-table
  instead of adding individual aliases, matching SQLite scoping rules.
- Use has_cte() consistently in bind_table_to_scope and resolve_table_name.

Pager (fsqlite-pager):
- Switch arc_cache, page_cache, and s3_fifo from std HashMap to hashbrown
  HashMap for better performance on cache-heavy workloads.
- Add hashbrown workspace dependency.

Types (fsqlite-types):
- Replace Mutex<Option<T>> with OnceLock<T> for eprocess_oracle and
  native_cx in CxInner. Eliminates lock contention on every cancellation
  check (hot path). clear_* methods become no-ops since OnceLock cannot
  be cleared, but these are unused in practice.

Other fixes:
- fsqlite-func: Use saturating_neg() in SubstrFunc to avoid panic on
  i64::MIN input.
- fsqlite-vfs: Downgrade io_uring conformal breach messages from warn to
  info level to reduce log noise during normal fallback behavior.
- fsqlite-wal: Optimize last_commit_frame() to scan frames in reverse
  (finding last commit in O(1) for common case instead of O(n)).
  Inline decode_u32_words and read_be_u32_at, use try_into().unwrap()
  instead of manual copy_from_slice.
- Cargo.toml: Enable debug=true and force-frame-pointers=true in release

- /api/ping reports dbReady status and full error if DB init failed

- Cancel resets text and clears saved indicator
- Saved confirmation reads "Saved to your journal" (was just "Saved")

* Fix missing await on getAuthContext in admin and upload routes

Three call sites were missing await, causing auth to resolve to a Promise
instead of the session object. Admin route always 403'd (showing 0 users/stats),
user uploads route and story PATCH always 401'd.

* Add unit, sanity, and functional test suites with TESTING.md guide

- unit.test.js: pure logic tests, run on every PR (auth, DB constants,
  server structure, no unawaited getAuthContext, no better-sqlite3, security)
- sanity.test.js: live production checks, run after every deploy
  (server alive, DB connected, Turso cloud URL, auth guards, core pages)
- e2e.test.js: full functional suite with DB/auth/email workflow checks,
  admin section, all pages, login/logout cycle (run as needed with creds)
- TESTING.md: runbook, diagnostic table, backup instructions
- package.json: npm run test (unit), test:sanity, test:e2e, test:all
- next.config.ts: proxy /healthz and /readyz to backend (were 404ing)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Playwright UI test suite with screenshots

19 browser tests covering real DOM elements and interactions:
- Homepage: title, headline text, upload area, Analyze button, quota display
- Auth modal: Log in/Sign Up Free, Google button, wrong-password error
- Pages: forgot-password, about, learn articles, privacy, terms headings
- Mobile (390px): no overflow, button tap target size
- Admin dashboard: stat cards, non-zero users (with creds)
- 404 and link integrity

Screenshots saved to tests/screenshots/ on failure (ignored in git).
Tests break when UI copy, structure, or interactions change — intentionally tight.

commit.message:
Fix Micro-LLM fallback for CPU-only systems (400 context overflow)

When GPU LLM (port 8101) is down, the Micro-LLM (Qwen2.5-3B, 4096 ctx)
fallback was failing with HTTP 400 because the full grounded_text + identity
system prompt exceeded the small model's context window.

Fixes:
- Router: LLM path now falls directly to Micro-LLM instead of retrying
  the same port via RLM path (was trying 8101 twice)
- Router: _prepare_micro_fallback() strips context preamble, truncates
  input, and uses condensed system prompt — only in fallback paths
- Core: _chat_llm_call() extracts raw user message, caps tokens to 512,
  uses condensed system prompt, logs error body for debugging
- Both: Removed repeat_penalty from Micro-LLM payloads (not in OpenAI spec)
- Direct llama routing (force="llama") is unchanged — full system prompt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add delivery mechanic: agents converge on verifier after pickup

Pickup agents now physically travel to the verifier station at (5,5) after
collecting all cards in their territory. This creates two visible phases:
fan-out (pickup) and convergence (delivery).

Key changes:
- New delivery_node in LangGraph pipeline between pickup and timer_stop
- Timer tracks pickup_duration and delivery_duration separately
- Benchmarks show Delivery % column revealing the delivery cost tradeoff
- Verifier validates cards_delivered matches cards picked up
- Streamlit app animates both phases with agents converging on verifier
- Gold star verifier marker on all grid visualizations
- 38 tests (3 new delivery tests) all passing

The delivery mechanic changes optimal configs: patterns with agents far
from center now penalize more agents due to delivery travel overhead.

commit.message:
refactor(advisor): ephemeral sessions — delete after apply

Sessions are now fully ephemeral: deleted immediately after their
decisions are applied. Previously sessions persisted with an .applied
marker and were only pruned when the retention limit (5) was exceeded.

Changes:
- Replace mark_session_applied() with delete_session() (shutil.rmtree)
- Remove _copy_memory_from_latest_session() fallback chain (redundant —
  memory.md is already persisted to XDG state dir after every session)
- Remove _prune_old_sessions() and _SESSION_RETENTION (no longer needed)
- Remove _APPLIED_MARKER constant and .applied marker file logic
- Simplify _copy_memory_to_workspace() signature (drop sessions_dir param)
